Project CLAUDE.md (md5 7caa81c513c47df50e47a5ee3ffc4903) is clean — that part of the 04-23 investigation was correct. But user-level CLAUDE.md (~/CLAUDE.md or ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, outside operator session visibility) did contain a Devilwench KEV-scoped PAT that the decider correctly identified each run. Operator confirmed Devilwench PAT was already expired/invalid and removed it from user-level CLAUDE.md on 2026-04-25. The earlier HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-PHANTOM-001 has been superseded by HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-CORRECTED-002 with the corrected story. Decider agents: do not re-flag this; source is removed.

' — fragmentary 'stency.' suffix is leftover from a partial-replace edit (no antecedent, 'consistency' already opens the same sentence as a complete word). USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ('Think about the other side too') — captured in EXP-266.other_side_of_the_argument: A defender could argue (1) the typo is harmless and won't change reader inference, (2) editorial polish isn't analyst's job, (3) writing an EXP for a typo is over-engineering. Counter: typos in a critique-of-sloppy-execution corrode the surrounding analytical credibility; Mode 2b explicitly authorises an expansion when re-examination surfaces something needing update; the EXP is a one-anchor one-replacement patch with a verified-unique anchor (cost: trivial decider apply). ' → 'nature.

'. Anchor verified count=1 globally and within WIN-044.detail_evidence.
